title: Pleudia santolinifolia

This classification places it as a member of the orchid family, which comprises the largest and most diverse group of flowering plants. intro: Pleudia santolinifolia, also known as Santolina, is a perennial shrub native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its silvery-gray foliage and yellow daisy-like flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which can help alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and soothe skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Pleudia santolinifolia include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Santolina is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, often used to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing.
